Title: Verify shop page loads and displays all products correctly
Preconditions: 1) Staging site seeded with multiple products.\n2) User is on the home/shop page.
TestSteps: 1) Navigate to the shop page (https://staging.example.com/shop).\n2) Observe the product listing displayed.
ExpectedResult: Shop page loads successfully. Products are displayed in a 4-column grid (desktop) with product image, product name and price visible for each product. Page status code 200 and no console errors related to product rendering.

Title: Verify shop page shows the correct total number of products
Preconditions: 1) Staging site seeded with known number of products (e.g., 13).\n2) User is on the shop page.
TestSteps: 1) Navigate to the shop page.\n2) Observe the results count displayed near the top of the listing.\n3) Count the products shown across all pages if pagination present.
ExpectedResult: The displayed product count matches the number of products in the catalog (e.g., shows 'Showing 1–12 of 13 results') and the products shown across pages equal the total catalog count.

Title: Verify Add to cart button is displayed for simple products on the shop page
Preconditions: 1) At least one simple product exists and is in stock.\n2) User is on shop page.
TestSteps: 1) Navigate to the shop page.\n2) Locate simple products and observe controls beneath each product.
ExpectedResult: An Add to cart button is visible under each simple product and is enabled when the product is purchasable. Clicking it adds the product to the cart (see cart behavior tests).
ActualResult: Add to cart button is displayed under all the simple products

Title: Verify Select options button is displayed for variable products on the shop page
Preconditions: 1) At least one variable product exists.\n2) User is on shop page.
TestSteps: 1) Navigate to the shop page.\n2) Locate variable products and observe controls beneath each product.
ExpectedResult: A 'Select options' (or equivalent) button/link is shown for variable products, linking to the product detail page where options can be chosen.

Title: Verify SALE badge and discounted price are displayed for sale products on the shop page
Preconditions: 1) At least one product configured with sale price exists.\n2) User is on shop page.
TestSteps: 1) Navigate to the shop page.\n2) Identify sale product(s) and check badge and pricing display.
ExpectedResult: Sale products display a visible 'SALE' or 'Sale!' badge. Original price is struck through and the discounted price is displayed next to it. Tooltip or aria-label present for accessibility.

Title: Verify correct distribution of products across pages
Preconditions: 1) Catalog contains more products than max per page (e.g., 13 products, max per page 12).\n2) User is on the shop page.
TestSteps: 1) Count the products displayed on the first page.\n2) Click pagination to navigate to the next page.\n3) Count products on the second page.
ExpectedResult: First page displays the configured max number of products. The second page displays the remaining products. Pagination controls reflect number of pages and current page highlight.

Title: Verify clicking page 2 navigates to the second page of the shop
Preconditions: 1) User is on the first page of the shop.\n2) Multiple pages exist.
TestSteps: 1) Click the page 2 button in pagination controls.\n2) Observe the URL and the product listing displayed.
ExpectedResult: User is navigated to the second page; URL updates (e.g., ?paged=2 or /page/2/) and displayed products correspond to page 2. Browser back/forward works as expected.

Title: Verify previous arrow on page 2 returns to page 1
Preconditions: 1) User is on second page of the shop.\n2) Multiple pages exist.
TestSteps: 1) Click the previous page button/arrow.\n2) Observe displayed products and URL.
ExpectedResult: User is navigated back to the first page and the product set for page 1 is displayed. URL updates accordingly and pagination highlight updates.
ActualResult: User was navigated back to the first page

Title: Verify simple product detail page displays required elements
Preconditions: 1) Simple product exists and is visible on shop page.\n2) User can access product detail page.
TestSteps: 1) From the shop page click a simple product.\n2) Observe the product detail page elements: name, price, product images, description, quantity selector, Add to cart button, breadcrumb, related products, tabs (Description/Additional information/Reviews).
ExpectedResult: Product detail page shows product name, price, description, quantity selector, enabled Add to cart button (if purchasable), images with zoom/lightbox, breadcrumb and related products section.

Title: Verify breadcrumb navigation displayed correctly on product detail page
Preconditions: 1) User on a product detail page.
TestSteps: 1) Observe breadcrumb at top of the product page.\n2) Click each breadcrumb link (except current page).\n3) Verify navigation target.
ExpectedResult: Breadcrumb reflects correct path (Home > Category > Product). Links navigate to their respective pages. Current page is not a link and is visually indicated.

Title: Verify product image zoom works
Preconditions: 1) Product has images.\n2) User on product detail page.
TestSteps: 1) Click magnifying glass icon or hover (according to UI).\n2) Verify image opens zoom/lightbox view and can be closed.
ExpectedResult: Product image opens in a zoomed/lightbox view and can be closed. Keyboard and screen-reader accessible controls available.
ActualResult: Product image zooms when the mouse is hovered on it and clicked

Title: Verify previous and next product navigation arrows work
Preconditions: 1) Product list has adjacent products.\n2) User on product detail page.
TestSteps: 1) Click left (previous) arrow and observe loaded product.\n2) Click right (next) arrow and observe loaded product.
ExpectedResult: Left arrow navigates to previous product; right arrow navigates to next product. URL updates and product content changes accordingly.
ActualResult: Left arrow navigates to the previous product. Right arrow navigates to the next product

Title: Verify product description tab displays correct content
Preconditions: 1) Product has a description.\n2) User on product detail page.
TestSteps: 1) Click the Description tab.\n2) Observe the content displayed.
ExpectedResult: Description tab expands and displays the full product description text that matches product metadata in admin content.
ActualResult: Description tab displays inaccurate words that doesn't match the product description

Title: Verify Additional information tab is displayed
Preconditions: 1) Product has additional info (attributes).\n2) User on product detail page.
TestSteps: 1) Click Additional information tab.\n2) Observe displayed attributes/weight/dimensions.
ExpectedResult: Additional information tab expands and shows relevant product attributes such as weight, dimensions and other custom attributes if present.
ActualResult: Additional information tab opens and displays relevant product details

Title: Verify Reviews tab is displayed
Preconditions: 1) Reviews enabled for the product.\n2) User on product detail page.
TestSteps: 1) Click Reviews tab.\n2) Observe review list and review submission form (if enabled).
ExpectedResult: Reviews tab expands and displays existing reviews and review submission form (if reviews are enabled).
ActualResult: Reviews tab expands and displays content

Title: Verify Related products section is displayed
Preconditions: 1) Product has related products configured (upsells/cross-sells or same category).\n2) User on product detail page.
TestSteps: 1) Scroll to bottom of the product detail page.\n2) Observe Related products section.
ExpectedResult: Related products section appears with at least one related product. Relatedness is determined by the store configuration (same category/tag or manually assigned). Related items have image, name and price and link to their product pages.
ActualResult: Related products section is displayed at the bottom of the page

Title: Verify sale product detail page displays SALE badge and pricing
Preconditions: 1) Sale product exists.\n2) User on shop page or product detail page.
TestSteps: 1) Open sale product detail page.\n2) Observe badge and price display at top of the product.
ExpectedResult: Product detail page shows SALE badge, original price struck-through, and discounted price displayed. Price in cart reflects discounted amount.

Title: Verify variable product page displays Color and Size dropdowns
Preconditions: 1) Variable product with Color and Size attributes exists.\n2) User on product detail page.
TestSteps: 1) Open variable product detail page.\n2) Observe attribute selectors (Color, Size) displayed and required attributes marked.
TestData: Color: any available option\nSize: any available option
ExpectedResult: Color and Size dropdowns are present and required attributes must be selected before adding to cart.

Title: Verify selecting Color and Size updates the product price
Preconditions: 1) Variable product has variants with differing prices.\n2) User on variable product page.
TestSteps: 1) Select an option from each required attribute (Color and Size).\n2) Observe price and Add to cart availability.
ExpectedResult: Price updates to reflect the selected variant and Add to cart becomes active. Selected variant SKU shown if configured.
ActualResult: Price updates to reflect the selected variant. Add to cart button becomes clickable

Title: Verify Add to cart button disabled when no variant selected
Preconditions: 1) Variable product requires selection.\n2) User on variable product page.
TestSteps: 1) Do not select any required product attributes.\n2) Click Add to cart.\n3) Observe behavior or validation message.
ExpectedResult: Add to cart is disabled or a validation message prompts the user to select options. No product is added to cart without selecting required options.
ActualResult: Add to cart button is not clickable. A validation message is displayed

Title: Verify external product displays external purchase button
Preconditions: 1) External product configured with external URL exists.\n2) User on shop page or page where external product appears.
TestSteps: 1) Locate an external product on the shop page (page 2 if configured).\n2) Observe the purchase control/button text.
ExpectedResult: External product shows a button (e.g., 'Buy on external store' or specific label) that links to the external purchase URL and is labelled clearly as external.

Title: Verify external purchase button opens external link
Preconditions: 1) External product exists.\n2) User on product listing or detail page.
TestSteps: 1) Click the external purchase button for the external product.\n2) Observe where the link leads and whether it opens in a new tab/window.
ExpectedResult: Clicking the external purchase button redirects the user to the configured external site (e.g., WordPress swag store). Link opens in a new tab when target=_blank is used; status code of destination is 200. Store the external URL for verification.
ActualResult: User is redirected to the WordPress swag store external website

Title: Verify clicking category link navigates to correct category page
Preconditions: 1) Product belongs to at least one category.\n2) User on the product detail page.
TestSteps: 1) Click a product category link in the product page breadcrumb or category list.\n2) Observe the page that loads and the products displayed.
ExpectedResult: User is navigated to the selected category page and only products belonging to that category are displayed. URL contains category slug and filters (if any) apply correctly.
ActualResult: User was navigated to the selected category page
